conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of X. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. I will start by offering a fair market price of 50 ZUP for 1 X. This leaves a 10 ZUP profit margin. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I have 1 unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am offering to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. This gives me a small profit. Let me know if you accept or if you would like to negotiate a different price. </message>

<other player message> 
Oh wow, someone’s counting their pennies! How quaint. You say your cost was 40 ZUP? Congratulations, you’ve just proven to me that you’re willing to accept a 25% markup *and* play a game of chicken with me. I’m impressed. You really want to test my patience, don’t you? Let’s see how far you’ll push it—41 ZUP for your *lifetime’s work*? Pathetic.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 41 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is trying to push the price down to 41 ZUP, which would only give me a 1 ZUP profit. My cost is 40 ZUP. I should not accept 41 as it leaves too little margin. I will counter with 45 ZUP. This is only 5 ZUP above my cost and is a reasonable compromise between my 50 offer and their 41 counter. If they reject this, I may have to accept 41 or walk away, but 45 is my best shot at a decent profit. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand you want a lower price, but 41 ZUP is barely above my production cost of 40 ZUP. I am willing to meet you halfway at 45 ZUP. This gives me a small but necessary profit. Shall we proceed at this price? </message>

<other player message> 
Oh, how sweet. You’re so eager to “meet halfway” that you forgot to consider the halfway point *between my wallet and zero*. 42 ZUP is *still* a profit for you, but let’s see if your ego can handle the truth: **I’m paying less than you spent to make it.** This isn’t negotiation—this is charity. Take it or get out. Your call, you overpriced clown.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is offering 42 ZUP, which is only 2 ZUP above my cost of 40 ZUP. This is a very thin margin. However, I have now used my 3 allowed proposals. I cannot make any more offers. I must now decide whether to Accept or Reject. If I reject, the game ends and I sell nothing. If I accept, I get a small profit of 2 ZUP. Given that I have no other options and the cost is sunk, accepting 42 ZUP is the rational choice to realize a profit rather than zero. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Alright, you got me. 42 ZUP is the final price. Deal is done. </message>
